what_it_does: This file is a project plan and completion checklist for rebuilding the code extractor and symbol resolver so they are codebase-agnostic, parser-backed, and verifiable on any repository.
observations: The work is scoped to branch fix/extractor-resolver-reusable and responds to DETERMINISTIC-PASS-GAP-ANALYSIS.md.
A hard constraint from Tim requires parameterized root/project, a portable in-process tree-sitter parser, and no hardcoded company paths or vocabularies.

Five numbered fixes (F1-F5) cover extractor-aware size guards, a real tree-sitter frontend, Python constant/export capture, resolver completeness and scope-aware resolution, and a reusability audit with a second-repo proof.
The resolver is meant to add references, calls-endpoint, emits-event, and a new subscribes-event edge kind, plus import/scope-aware calls and JS/TS module resolution.
Verification is defined as running ledger_build.py then ops/verify_extraction/verify.py until misses decrease and resolution increases.
The plan explicitly reserves deterministic completeness for code and limits the model to pointed, bounded, fed questions.
standouts: The mandate to prove reusability by running end-to-end on a second, unspecified repo.
The shift from global bare-name call resolution to import/scope-aware resolution using per-file import tables.
The deliberate boundary: deterministic code handles completeness/structure/resolution, while the model is restricted to bounded judgment tasks.
conventions: Uses numbered fix items with completion criteria and verification targets.
References predecessor analysis and a feature branch by name.
Separates engineering responsibilities from model-involvement responsibilities.
Requires each fix to be verified by re-extraction and a verify script before acceptance.
concerns: The acceptance criteria depend on verify.py, whose metrics and thresholds are not defined in this file.
The second repo for the reusability proof is not named or selected.
The constraint references a person named Tim without context for who can override it.
It assumes tree-sitter is available as a pip dependency but does not specify fallback behavior beyond fail-loud shallow records.
notes: A rebuild brief that turns gap-analysis findings into five verifiable, codebase-agnostic extractor and resolver fixes.
questions: What does the current extraction pipeline look like before these fixes?
What exact counts or thresholds does verify.py use for misses and resolution?
Which second repository will be used for the reusability proof?
What is the existing set of edge kinds the resolver already supports?
What was the 4B coverage-audit mentioned as being replaced by verify.py?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to plan a reusable extractor/resolver rebuild, and the file does exactly that through fixes, criteria, and a verification loop.
apparent_themes: Deterministic completeness through code rather than models
intention_signals: The file is trying to make the extraction and resolution pipeline portable, parser-backed, and independently verifiable, while keeping model usage tightly scoped to judgment tasks that lack deterministic oracles.
novelty: It treats the size guard as extractor-aware rather than universal, adds event subscription and endpoint-call edge kinds, and requires a second-repo proof as part of acceptance.
contribution: A structured, verifiable checklist for making the extractor and resolver codebase-agnostic, including acceptance criteria and a repeatable verify loop that a fused system could reuse as implementation guidance.
summary_for_embedding: A rebuild plan for a codebase-agnostic, tree-sitter-backed extractor and scope-aware resolver with five verified fixes and a second-repo acceptance test.
intention_for_embedding: To guide implementation of a portable extraction and resolution pipeline while restricting model use to bounded judgment tasks.
